intent: Stop pubspec.yaml's adaptive_icon_background from drifting from style.BACKGROUND -- it just did, across all 6 apps, and the drift was invisible until a physical device check.
changes: Added sync_pubspec_background(app): rewrites pubspec.yaml's adaptive_icon_background line in place to match style.BACKGROUND, called automatically from run_flutter_launcher_icons before invoking the dart tool

Root cause found during the warm-palette rollout: style.py's BACKGROUND was updated, but each app's pubspec.yaml carries its own independent adaptive_icon_background literal (flutter_launcher_icons' own config format) that nothing kept in sync -- all 6 apps stayed on the old value even though every generated PNG asset was correct. Android's actual home-screen icon is the adaptive icon (background color + foreground drawable), not the legacy ic_launcher.png mipmap, so this was invisible in file-byte checks and only showed up on the physical device

verification.evidence: 43 passed (up from 39; 4 new tests for sync_pubspec_background)

verification.command: PYTHONPATH=~/testsAndMisc python3 -m python_pkg.app_icons generate --android; grep adaptive_icon_background across all 6 pubspec.yaml
verification.evidence: All 6 now read #211D1B; diet-guard rebuilt + reinstalled on physical device, home-screen icon background pixel-sampled at (33,29,27) = #211D1B

risks: The regex-based line rewrite assumes flutter_launcher_icons' pubspec.yaml block keeps adaptive_icon_background on its own line in `key: "#RRGGBB"` form -- if that config format ever changes, the sync silently becomes a no-op rather than erroring (the function returns False for 'already matches' and 'pattern didn't match' identically). A follow-up could distinguish those two cases if this recurs.
rollback: git revert this commit in testsAndMisc
Each app repo's own pubspec.yaml/icon-asset commit can be reverted independently
